title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation · History Brief: Gita Autosquash Merges Update Refs
summary: A stacked change series must move between release bases, preserve selected merges, reorder dependent commits, and satisfy a review policy without losing authorship or test evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita autosquash merges update refs increment in rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate autosquash, rebase-merges, update-refs, autostash, empty-commit, and backend tradeoffs against collaboration policy.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-autosquash-merges-update-refs — Evaluate autosquash, rebase-merges, update-refs, autostash, empty-commit, and backend tradeoffs against collaboration policy.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 3
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity
git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
# expected: range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries
# observation: four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review
# changed-case: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass
# transfer-limit: coordinate real shared-history rewrites and publish with an explicit lease expectation
